title: Nazzri
content:
{{Top|leg}}

'''Nazzri''' was a [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that was located in the [[Lantillian sector/Legends|Lantillian sector]] and was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Nazzar/Legends|Nazzar]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. The planet had become a member of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] by [[17,000 BBY]] and was one of the worlds that provided it with [[Army/Legends|armies]] during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] of [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]. Around that [[Time/Legends|time]], the [[Nazzar government]], which was originally a [[Monarchy/Legends|monarchy]], and [[Emperor-King]] [[Prrit Qabaq]] of Nazzri, had became corrupted by the use of [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark]] [[Magic/Legends|magic]]. Eventually, the return of Qabaq's daughter, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Ikree]], to Nazzri from a [[Boqeri|self-imposed exile]] precipitated a [[Nazzri civil war|civil war]] between the government and opposing rebels. Qabaq was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in [[Battle on Nazzri|battle]], and Ikree became the first [[Monarch-praefect|queen-praefect]] of a new [[Theocracy|theocratic]] government based on the [[Ulizra]] religion.

Approximately a [[Millennium/Legends|millennium]] later, Crown Prince [[Qrrrl Toq]] of Nazzri learned about the circumstances of the Nazzri monarchy's downfall while researching the [[Great Structure|tenets]] of Ulizra. As a result, Toq decided to embark upon an unprecedented off-world journey of his own, which eventually led [[Gender/Legends|him]] to become a [[Jedi/Legends|member]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. As a consequence of the principles of Ulizra that dominated the Nazzar society, over time the species became largely [[Xenophobia|xenophobic]] and incurious about the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], and following the [[Declaration of a New Order|rise]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the population of Nazzri withdrew from [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] affairs into isolationism.

==Description==
Nazzri was a terrestrial<ref name="TEA" /> [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TOTJC" /> located in the [[Nazzri system/Legends|Nazzri system]].<ref name="TEA" /> A [[Temperature/Legends|temperate]] and verdant world<ref name="UAA" /> with rolling plains on its surface, Nazzri was situated in the [[Lantillian sector/Legends|Lantillian sector]], a part of the [[Kashyyyk Region]] in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="TEA" />

The planet lay on the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] known as the [[Lesser Lantillian Route]], which linked it to the [[Avenelle system/Legends|Avenelle system]] as well as the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Vena system/Legends|Vena system]].<ref name="TEA" /> By approximately [[17,000 BBY]], an additional [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] route connected Nazzri to the [[Gizer system/Legends|Gizer system]],<ref name="EGTW" /> and another hyperlane linked it to the [[Kashyyyk system/Legends|Kashyyyk system]] by [[3976 BBY]]. Those routes persisted until [[3956 BBY]] but had ceased to exist by [[1004 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
===Decline of a Republic world===
[[File:NazzriAlsakanConflicts-EGTW.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Nazzri was a part of the Galactic Republic by 17,000 BBY.]]
[[Realspace/Legends|Space]] surrounding Nazzri was [[Scout/Legends|explored]] between [[20,000 BBY/Legends|20,000 BBY]] and [[15,000 BBY/Legends|15,000 BBY]],<ref name="TEA" /> and the planet had become a member of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] by approximately 17,000 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]]. The hyperlanes that Nazzri was situated on during the subsequent [[12,000 BBY/Legends|five millennia]] were among those that were either neutral or contested in a long [[Alsakan Conflicts/Legends|series]] of [[Politics/Legends|political]] conflicts that arose in that [[Time/Legends|time]] period between the Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world, [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the planet [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]], and their respective allies. In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], when the Republic fought the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] against the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], Nazzri was one of the outlier worlds that provided the Republic with [[Army/Legends|armies]] of relatively primitive tribal [[Spear/Legends|spearmen]].<ref name="EGTW" />

[[Millennium/Legends|Millennia]] prior to<ref name="TOTJC" /> [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]],<ref name="TOTJCdate">''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' is set 4,000 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which translates to [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the planet's native [[Nazzar/Legends|Nazzar]] [[Species/Legends|species]] formed a [[Nazzar government|government]] that was a [[Monarchy/Legends|monarchy]] centered on the position of [[Emperor-King]]. That government had been corrupted at least a thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to<ref name="TOTJC" /> 4000 BBY<ref name="TOTJCdate" /> through the use of ancient rites of<ref name="TOTJC" /> [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark]] [[Magic/Legends|magic]]<ref name="CSWE Ikree" /> discovered by the last Emperor-King, [[Prrit Qabaq]], leading to a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] of economic decline, widespread [[Diet/Legends|starvation]], and strife, including the persecution of any Nazzar who dared to speak out against the regime.<ref name="TOTJC" />

===Daughter against father===
Eventually, Qabaq's daughter, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Ikree]], returned to Nazzri from a twelve year–long<ref name="CSWE Ikree" /> self-imposed [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] known as [[boqeri]]. [[Gender/Legends|She]] began speaking out against the actions of the planet's government, which prompted Qabaq—who was unaware of the agitator's identity—to order that she be immediately arrested and brought before him, whereupon the Emperor-King was surprised to learn that the rebel had been his own daughter.<ref name="TOTJC" />

Over the subsequent [[Standard month/Legends|months]], Qabaq and Ikree engaged in continuous verbal sparring, each condemning the other's words and actions.<ref name="TOTJC" /> Ikree's arrest split Nazzri's population,<ref name="CSWE Ikree" /> leading to a period of unrest throughout the planet<ref name="TOTJC" /> and eventually a protracted [[Nazzri civil war|civil war]].<ref name="CSWE Ikree" /> Qabaq ordered a planet-wide martial law, which only served to further populate the ranks of the Nazzar rebels. Ultimately, the Nazzar government's military faced the rebels in a massive [[Battle on Nazzri|battle]] during which Ikree was forced to [[Death/Legends|kill]] Qabaq.<ref name="TOTJC" />

===A new era===
In the aftermath of the conflict, Ikree revealed that during her boqeri, she had discovered the [[Great Structure]], a system of beliefs and teachings that governed the [[Ulizra]] religion. The princess proposed the formation of a new form of [[Theocracy|theocratic]] planetary government that would be based on the tenets of the Great Structure and would infuse the Nazzar society with a code of ethics and morals and prevent the repeat of the atrocities committed during her [[Parent/Legends|father's]] reign. Ikree then ascended to the newly-created position of [[Monarch-praefect|queen-praefect]] of Nazzri, and her first act was to order the destruction of all records of Qabaq so as to prevent the possibility of anyone else using the dark rites he had discovered. [[Unidentified book (Prrit Qabaq)|One compilation]] of documents focusing on the last Emperor-King, however, survived the purge and came to rest in the building known as the [[Hall of Edification]].<ref name="TOTJC" />

Although initially the new government worked well, with every Nazzar enjoying complete freedom, only half a year after Ikree had assumed the Nazzri [[Throne/Legends|throne]] a fanatical [[Unidentified Nazzar noble|noble]] of Qabaq's court murdered the [[Queen/Legends|queen]]-[[praefect]]. After Ikree's death, the Nazzar government progressively became more strict in adhering to the laws of Ulizra at any cost, in the process limiting the rights of individuals.<ref name="TOTJC" />

===The descendant===
[[File:QrrrlToq-TSW4.png|thumb|left|220px|Crown Prince Qrrl Toq of Nazzri entered self-imposed exile upon learning of the circumstances of the downfall of his homeworld's ancient monarchy.]]
Around a millennium after Qabaq's death, the Emperor-King's descendant [[Qrrrl Toq]] was the son of Nazzri's reigning [[King/Legends|king]] and the crown prince of the planet. Supervised by several teachers, Toq studied the fourteen constituent aspects—known as [[ven]]s—of the Great Structure, spending exactly one [[local year]] on each of them. Eventually, the crown prince's study performance gave rise to rumors that he would go on to become the first king-praefect since the establishment of Nazzri's theocracy. Such hopes made Toq intensify his research, with the Nazzar believing that his homeworld's population had left behind the original goals of the Ulizra-based government and that it was his destiny to re-establish that religion's foundational beliefs and [[Philosopher/Legends|philosophy]]. In Toq's mind, such an accomplishment would help bring about a state of worldwide peace unprecedented since the theocracy's establishment.<ref name="TOTJC" />

On one of Toq's study visits to the Hall of Edification, the crown prince discovered the [[Book/Legends|volume]] on Qabaq. Reading through the book in two and a half [[Standard day/Legends|days]], Toq learned about the circumstances of the Nazzri monarchy's downfall and decided that instead of attempting to match the achievements of prior monarch-praefects, he needed to discover and follow his own way. Subsequently—similarly to his ancestor Ikree—the Nazzar left his homeworld for the first time in his life, embarking on a boqeri of his own while privately wondering whether he would ever return. Three years later, Toq encountered off-world members of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], a meeting which eventually led to the crown prince becoming a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] himself,<ref name="TOTJC" /> and the Nazzar ultimately [[Battle of Kemplex Nine|perished]] in service to the Order during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]<ref name="TSW4">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 4]]</ref> of [[3996 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" />

===Isolationism===
Between [[3959 BBY]] and 3956 BBY, Nazzri was situated in the territory of [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire]]. The planet fell within the borders of the Republic once again by the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] of [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]–[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="TEA" /> during the first campaigns of which both the [[Fourth Outer Rim Army|Fourth]] and the [[Twelfth Army/Legends|Twelfth Sector Army]] of the Republic were charged with engaging the [[Confederacy military/Legends|military forces]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] in Nazzri's vicinity.<ref name="EGTW" />

Although Nazzri was located in space controlled by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] by [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]],<ref name="EGTW" /> upon the Empire's [[Declaration of a New Order|formation]] what few of the [[Xenophobia|xenophobic]] Nazzar remained off-world mostly returned to the [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] controlled by the species' government. The Nazzar then proceeded to ignore [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] at large, an attitude which persisted<ref name="UAA" /> by [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]].<ref name="NJOera">''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'' establishes that the isolationist attitude of the [[Nazzar/Legends|Nazzar]] continued into the [[New Jedi Order era]], which the [[Wookieepedia:The Star Wars Legends Novels Timeline|''Star Wars'' Legends Novels Timeline]] dates to between [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] and [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]].</ref> Furthermore, Nazzri had yet to accept invitations to become a part of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]<ref name="UAA" /> despite the planet already being situated within the territory of that government by [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]].<ref name="EGTW" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Nazzar/Legends}}
===Religious equines===
[[File:Nazzar.png|thumb|right|220px|Nazzri was the homeworld of the Nazzar species.]]
Nazzri was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the Nazzar, a species of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]]<ref name="TOTJC" /> [[Equine/Legends|equine]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]]<ref name="CSWE Nazzar">{{CSWECite|II|364|Nazzar}}</ref> that spoke the [[Nazzar (language)|Nazzar]] [[Language/Legends|language]].<ref name="UAA" /> Around 25 ABY, the population of the Nazzri system numbered between one and ten million.<ref name="TEA" />

The Nazzar were nearly fanatical adherents of Ulizra,<ref name="TOTJC" /> the ancient,<ref name="CSWE Ulizra">{{CSWECite|III|278|Ulizra}}</ref> state-enforced religion of Nazzri. The main tenets of Ulizra were: the destruction of any other philosophical or religious systems that undermined the Nazzar religion; seclusion from the rest of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]—since outsiders were likely to taint the Nazzar culture—and the main principle, the deeming of the society as being of higher importance than any individual. The Nazzar used the laws of Ulizra to guide all of their decisions.<ref name="TOTJC" />

As a result, the species largely exhibited<ref name="TOTJC" /> mild xenophobia,<ref name="UAA" /> with all off-worlders being viewed as potentially ideologically dangerous and therefore often receiving unfriendly welcomes from Nazzar officials,<ref name="TOTJC" /> and lacked curiosity about the wider galaxy.<ref name="TEA" /> Occasionally, however, Nazzar who had traveled beyond their homeworld formed friendships with members of other species and thus managed to overcome their natural prejudices.<ref name="UAA" />

===Planetary government===
Although initially the Nazzri government had been a monarchy, it was eventually abandoned in favor of a [[Oligarchy|oligarchical]] theocracy. The government,<ref name="TOTJC" /> which by 4000 BBY<ref name="TOTJCdate" /> had spread beyond the Nazzri system and controlled three neighboring systems, was headed by a king and fourteen praefects, or the heads of Ulizra's fourteen sects.<ref name="TOTJC" />

The king presided over the council of the praefects and [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanded]] both Nazzri's military forces and all of its planetary security agencies. Only the praefects, however, could vote on the use of the military in offensive actions as well as introduce and vote on potential laws and changes and amendments to the state-given rights of the Nazzar. Most of the planet's population could predict the outcome of any such vote, for the praefects, like the rest of the species, adhered to the tenets of Ulizra while making their decisions.<ref name="TOTJC" />

===Off-world Nazzar===
Off-world Nazzar were an uncommon sight<ref name="UAA" /> and largely belonged to one of two categories: fanatical preachers of Ulizra that sought to spread the Nazzar religion elsewhere in the galaxy; and exiles. The latter had usually either committed a violation of one of the Ulizran moral codes, such as by the defilement of a temple, or outright rejected the religion's teachings.<ref name="TOTJC" />

Very rarely, Nazzar in good standing with Nazzri's Ulizran authorities left the planet on a boqeri, a self-imposed exile of varied length, in order to seek spiritual revelations. Such travelers often found themselves drawn to adventure and tended to be [[Noble/Legends|nobles]], scouts, [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], or practitioners of [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-using organizations. Overall, many of the Nazzar who left Nazzri found the wider galaxy to be too fascinating to ever consider returning to their homeworld.<ref name="UAA" />

==Locations==
Prrit Qabaq, the last Emperor-King of the Nazzar, ruled from a palace on Nazzri<ref name="CSWE Ikree" /> that included a throne chamber. Later, Ikree, Qabaq's daughter and the planet's first queen-praefect, used the palace as her seat of power, and the structure was still in use at least a millennium later.<ref name="TOTJC" /> In addition, all the historical records of the Nazzar were kept in an ancient building known as the Hall of Edification.<ref name="CSWE Hall">{{CSWECite|II|10|Hall of Edification}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Tales_of_the_Jedi_Companion.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Nazzri was introduced in the ''Tales of the Jedi Companion''.]]
Nazzri was first mentioned in the [[1996]] [[roleplaying game]] sourcebook ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'', written by [[George R. Strayton]] and published by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="TOTJC" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed it in grid square P-9.<ref name="TEA" />

In [[2008]], the entry for Princess Ikree in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' presented an account of the Nazzri monarchy's downfall<ref name="CSWE Ikree" /> that largely contradicts that contained in ''Tales of the Jedi Companion''.<ref name="TOTJC" /> In the former, the verbal sparring between Ikree and Emperor-King Prrit Qabaq precedes Ikree's public speaking out against Qabaq's regime and her subsequent arrest.<ref name="CSWE Ikree" />

In addition, the entry claims that the religion of Ulizra was already practiced by the Nazzar before the downfall of Qabaq's government and that the murder of Ikree occurred prior to the end of the Nazzar civil war and the establishment of Nazzri's new, Ulizra-based government. This article incorporates details from ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' wherever they do not contradict the account included in ''Tales of the Jedi Companion'' but otherwise treats the later source as erroneous.<ref name="CSWE Ikree" />
